code.text: Coagulation

text.div:
Coagulation

Test09-MAR-2021Reference RangeUnit
Fibrin D-dimer FEU [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma0.30 - 0.5ug/mL
Prothrombin time (PT)12.311.0 - 13.0s
INR in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay3.0 H0.9 - 1.2{INR}

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows marked hypoxemia, anemia, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, and abnormal white cell differential (monocytosis, basophilia, low neutrophil and lymphocyte percentages). There is severe hyperbilirubinemia, elevated creatinine (renal dysfunction), high ferritin, and a markedly elevated INR (coagulopathy). These findings suggest multi-organ dysfunction, likely with hepatic and renal impairment, possible bone marrow suppression, and a high risk of bleeding. Immediate clinical evaluation is recommended.
